sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The M square at (39.5, 35.5) moved to (38.5, 34.5). That's row-1, col-1. So clicking M color also moves the M square up-left, same as S color.
Wait, but I clicked the M color square at (1.5, 5.5) and then the M square at (39, 35). The M square moved from (39.5, 35.5) to (38.5, 34.5), which is row-1, col-1.
So both S and M colors move the M square up-left? That doesn't make sense. Let me reconsider.
Actually, wait. Maybe the color square I clicked doesn't determine the direction. Maybe clicking ANY color square selects that color, and then clicking an M square moves it in a direction determined by the M square's position relative to the blue blob.
Or maybe the direction is always the same (up-left) regardless of the color clicked. Let me test by clicking a different color and seeing if the direction changes.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The M squares are at:
- (34.5, 16.5) - needs to move up-right to reach (27, 33)
- (38.5, 34.5) - needs to move up-left to reach (27, 33)
- (55.5, 15.5) - needs to move up-right to reach (27, 33)
- (55.5, 45.5) - needs to move up-left to reach (27, 33)
The M square at (38.5, 34.5) moved up-left (row-1, col-1), which is the correct direction to reach the blue blob at (27, 33).
The M square at (34.5, 16.5) also moved up-left when I clicked S, but it needs to move up-right to reach the blue blob. So maybe the direction is not determined by the color, but by the M square's position relative to the blue blob.
Wait, but the M square at (34.5, 16.5) moved from (35.5, 17.5) to (34.5, 16.5), which is up-left. But the blue blob is at (27, 33), which is up and to the right from (35.5, 17.5). So the M square moved in the wrong direction!
Hmm, maybe the goal is not to move the M squares to the blue blob. Maybe the goal is something else.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the colors correspond to directions:
- S → up-left (row-1, col-1)
- M → up-right (row-1, col+1)
- p → down-left (row+1, col-1)
- Y → down-right (row+1, col+1)
But both S and M caused up-left movement. Let me test p and Y to see if they cause different movements.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. When I clicked S color (at 1, 1) and then the M square at (35, 17), the M square moved from (35.5, 17.5) to (34.5, 16.5). That's row-1, col-1.
When I clicked M color (at 1, 5) and then the M square at (39, 35), the M square moved from (39.5, 35.5) to (38.5, 34.5). That's also row-1, col-1.
So both S and M colors cause up-left movement. Maybe the color doesn't determine the direction, but clicking any color square "activates" the movement mode, and clicking an M square moves it towards the blue blob.
But the M square at (35.5, 17.5) moved up-left, away from the blue blob at (27, 33). So maybe the movement is not towards the blue blob.
Let me test by clicking p color and seeing if the direction changes.
